# Finance and Risk Management

## Credit Risk
Credit risk is the possibility that a borrower or counterparty fails to meet financial obligations. A bank evaluates credit risk by reviewing repayment history, current debt burden, collateral, and economic conditions. Strong controls include underwriting standards, portfolio limits, and periodic review of credit quality.

## Portfolio Hedging
Portfolio hedging reduces exposure to adverse market movements by using offsets or derivatives. Hedging strategies are designed to control volatility while preserving expected upside. They are especially useful when firms need to stabilize earnings or protect funding positions.

## Regulatory Reporting
Financial institutions must produce accurate reports for regulators, auditors, and shareholders. Regulatory reporting depends on reliable data governance, reconciliations, and timely controls. Effective reporting systems minimize errors and reduce the risk of material misstatements.
